## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, next up we have continuity and this is continuity is you might or might not have heard that term. That's basically where people pay you ongoing. So they might pay you a month. Typically it's monthly. They'll pay you a monthly fee and it could be they could be paying you $8 to be part of your membership site or they could pay you, you know, $1,000 a month to be part of your mastermind group or something. I've done, well I haven't done $8 a month. I've certainly done $1,000 a month. I've actually done, actually I've done $6,000 a month programs. So continuity is where they pay you an ongoing basis to be part of your program and to learn from you and get your materials. Lots of different ways to do this. You can do a membership site and we'll get into all the, you know, some of the nitty-gritty details on this in just a minute. You know, you can have a membership site where you're posting new content, new videos, new audios, new PDFs where there's a forum or whatever. There's a lot of different types of membership sites. It could be a training course, a fixed thing, like they pay you monthly for one year and they get every month they get a new, you know, a new set of whatever set of videos, set of audios, a new chunk of content every month. It could be a newsletter. This could be a physical newsletter that you send out via mail, snail mail, or FedEx. It could be done online or it could be done both ways. I get an investing newsletter that I pay for and I pay for it every year I pay for it and it's available online but they also send it to me via the mail. The continuity, it could be like a CD or DVD of the month club type of thing. Now the thing about continuity is it can be really awesome because basically you make the sale once and then people keep on paying you. You basically keep on charging their credit cards typically every month, sometimes it's every two weeks, sometimes it's even every week, but typically every month you'll charge them. The great thing about this is, you know, once you make that sale you keep on getting paid over and over and over. Now I remember when I first, I mean continuity was the thing I started with right from the very beginning. I published, you know, a subscription service where people paid me on a monthly or a quarterly or a yearly basis to get my information about the stock market. I was actually sending out the information via email so I was getting paid, it was basically $97 a month to get email from me and my only cost of delivery was, you know, to have an email service that would send out my emails. So it was awesome. And when I first started doing this I'm like, boy, someone pays me maybe $7 a month. They're going to pay me, you know, if I add like, you know, if I find 20 people to pay me in that first year or first month I'll make $2,000 and then if I can add 20 people each month then like by the end of the second month I'm making $4,000 a month and by the

## [03:01 - 06:03]

end of the third month I'm making $6,000 a month and by the end of the fourth month I'm making $8,000 a month and by the end of the year when I'm making $24,000 a month and I started doing this math I got really excited. That was the most amazing thing. The thing I forgot about was that people drop out. So you know, basically you have to have a way to continually add new people into your program because people will not stay with you forever. You know, the matter, you will have some raving fans that are just going to stick and they'll stick and they'll stick and they'll stick and they'll stick and they'll keep on paying you. But that's a really small, just a small chunk of people. So you have to go through a lot of people, add a lot of people into your program to get to the point where you've got a lot of people that are in like lifers we call them, people that are, you know, in, they're going to stick with you through thick and thin, they're going to be with you forever. Those are the people you live and die for but you have to sell to a lot of other people. You know, a lot of programs you might stick someone if it's a monthly program for three or four months. You know, that with a lot of programs it's very average. So you have to continually add new people. A lot of times someone will build up a list and they'll have, you know, 3,000 people in a list and like, I'll launch a continuity program and since it's a new fresh list, they get like 5% of the people joined so they add 3,000 people on their list and they get 5% that's 150 people. And they're like, wow, this is awesome. And they think, I'll get 150 people that pay me every single month. But the problem is that you have to keep on adding new people because after three months that 150 people will be down to like 30 or 40 people. That's just what happens with continuity. So it's a great, it can be a great, I don't want to discourage you from continuity. It's a fantastic way to do business. It's a fantastic way to build ongoing revenue but you have to continually add new people and you renewal rate. How often people renew their first, you know, if they pay for a month, how many renew at the end of that first month and then how many renew after two months, those are some really important numbers. Those will be the numbers that you work on in your business and the number that you focus most on in your business as you go forward. Next up we have home study courses. This is like, you know, almost going to be old school now in a lot of markets. This is where you send out people, send them like, you know, a bunch of DVDs and maybe a couple books for them to go through. And you know, even three or four, five, six years ago, this was the way to go for high end products. You shipped people a box of stuff. It's a box of DVDs, CDs, books, manuals, cheat sheets, you know, laminated, flow charts and all kinds of stuff. Lot less of that going on these days. They're really not, I've created, you know, a few different home study courses. It can be a lot of work to put together, but you know, it's, you know, one piece at a time. You know, you shoot some videos, record a couple of audios, you know, write some reports and then you get all that stuff created by someone else. We'll talk about that in a bit.

## [06:03 - 08:46]

You know, you get the stuff created by like a fulfillment house. But you know, there's less of this going on, but still it can be a great way to go. This is the way you start to get higher prices. You can get $300. You can get, you know, $500. You can get $1,000. You can get $2,000. So I've sold home study products anywhere from $500 to $2,000 and worked really, really well. You know, it is more complicated. You have to ship out product. You have to worry about, you know, the shipping. Even if you're not physically shipping it, you have to worry about it getting lost in transit. You have to worry about people, you know, returning or people telling you it was damaged or stuff like that. But it can be a really good way to go. And then sort of the next evolution what we've seen recently is coaching programs. And this is typically online coaching programs. And with online coaching programs, people might get, you know, they might get downloadable audios, downloadable videos. They get, you know, action guides. They could get PDFs. They could get all kinds of, you know, different stuff, but it's downloadable. And typically with a coaching program, there'll be some phone calls, some either one-on-one phone calls or some group phone calls or webinars. So you're mixing different formats typically. You'll typically, not always, but when I do it, you know, I want people to get the best results. So I'll give them a lot of them's video. I'll give them audio. There'll be, you know, mind maps. There'll be cheat sheets. There'll be action guides, stuff like that. And coaching programs can be very effective in terms of helping people out, in terms of them getting results. What I've found is that people a lot of times with these online coaching programs get better results than you do with home study courses. Because the home study course, it comes, they get that box and they put it on the shelf and they never even open it up. With the coaching program, there's this ongoing element, you know, there's maybe webinars, tele-seminars that you can add in. And they can be really, really effective. And one of the things I want to mention, and I think I've already mentioned this, is you can mix and match all of these together. So I've actually done online coaching programs where you also did send them out, basically a home study portion of it. Or after the program's over, then we bundled up into a home study course, so they end up getting both. I've done online coaching programs where they got live events. And I've done them where they got tele-seminars, tele-seminars and webinars. So you can definitely mix and match among these different formats. But these are some of the major formats that we're seeing right now that have been really, really effective.
